machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin ring (outer rings of basal body)
machinery_genes.quote: InvG (a member of the secretin family), PrgH, and PrgK, were part of this complex
machinery_genes.note: Kimbrough & Miller 2000 PNAS; InvG forms the outer-membrane rings of the SPI-1 needle complex. The secretin role is corroborated by Lou 2019 (PMID 31428589): 'Three proteins, InvG, which comprises the outer rings; PrgH, and PrgK, which are thought to form the rest of the structure'.

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane lipoprotein/pilotin required for InvG secretin ring assembly
machinery_genes.quote: InvG, a member of the PulD family of pore forming outer membrane proteins (secretins), forms an oligomeric ring structure in E. coli when expressed with InvH
machinery_genes.note: Kimbrough & Miller 2000; InvH is the pilotin/accessory lipoprotein needed for InvG (secretin) oligomerization and outer-membrane localization in the SPI-1 needle complex.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ring of the basal body (MS-ring)
machinery_genes.quote: PrgH and PrgK, in the absence of other TTSS components, oligomerize into ring-shaped structures identical in appearance and size to the base of the NC, indicating that they are likely the major inner membrane structural components required for secretion
machinery_genes.note: Kimbrough & Miller 2000; PrgH encoded in the prgHIJK operon, multimerizes and provides the structural foundation for PrgK oligomerization in the SPI-1 needle complex base.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ring lipoprotein of the basal body
machinery_genes.quote: PrgH and PrgK, in the absence of other TTSS components, oligomerize into ring-shaped structures identical in appearance and size to the base of the NC, indicating that they are likely the major inner membrane structural components required for secretion
machinery_genes.note: Kimbrough & Miller 2000; PrgK has a canonical lipoprotein acylation signal sequence and, like FliF, multimerizes to form part of the basal structure of the SPI-1 needle complex (SctJ inner ring).

machinery_genes.role: needle subunit (main component of the external needle filament)
machinery_genes.quote: PrgI and PrgJ are required for full NC assembly and are likely components of the external needle
machinery_genes.note: Kimbrough & Miller 2000; PrgI cofractionates with the needle complex and is secreted. Lou 2019 (PMID 31428589) states 'PrgI is the main component of the needle portion'.

machinery_genes.role: inner rod of the basal body
machinery_genes.quote: PrgJ forms an inner rod within the basal body
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019 SPI-1 review; PrgJ encoded in the prgHIJK operon and assembles as the inner rod connecting the basal body to the needle. Also documented as a secreted NC component by Kimbrough & Miller 2000 (PMID 10984518).

machinery_genes.quote: The export apparatus is composed of the proteins InvA, SpaP, SpaQ, SpaR, and SpaS
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; InvA is the SctV-family core export-apparatus component of the SPI-1 T3SS inner membrane.

machinery_genes.quote: The export apparatus is composed of the proteins InvA, SpaP, SpaQ, SpaR, and SpaS
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; SpaP is the SctR export-gate component of the SPI-1 export apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: The export apparatus is composed of the proteins InvA, SpaP, SpaQ, SpaR, and SpaS
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; SpaQ is the SctS export-gate component of the SPI-1 export apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: The export apparatus is composed of the proteins InvA, SpaP, SpaQ, SpaR, and SpaS
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; SpaR is the SctT export-gate component of the SPI-1 export apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: The export apparatus is composed of the proteins InvA, SpaP, SpaQ, SpaR, and SpaS
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; SpaS is the SctU-family export-gate autoprotease (substrate-specificity switch) of the SPI-1 export apparatus.

machinery_genes.quote: The sorting platform consists of five proteins, SpaO, OrgA, OrgB, InvI, and the hexameric ATPase InvC
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; SpaO is the SctQ sorting-platform (C-ring) component of the SPI-1 T3SS.

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic sorting platform / C-ring base component
machinery_genes.quote: The sorting platform consists of five proteins, SpaO, OrgA, OrgB, InvI, and the hexameric ATPase InvC
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; OrgA is the SctK sorting-platform base component of the SPI-1 T3SS.

machinery_genes.quote: The sorting platform consists of five proteins, SpaO, OrgA, OrgB, InvI, and the hexameric ATPase InvC
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; OrgB is the SctL stator/C-ring link tethering the InvC ATPase to the sorting platform of the SPI-1 T3SS.

machinery_genes.quote: The sorting platform consists of five proteins, SpaO, OrgA, OrgB, InvI, and the hexameric ATPase InvC
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; InvI is the SctO ATPase central-stalk component of the SPI-1 sorting platform.

machinery_genes.quote: The sorting platform consists of five proteins, SpaO, OrgA, OrgB, InvI, and the hexameric ATPase InvC
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; InvC is the SctN-family hexameric secretion ATPase of the SPI-1 T3SS (originally characterized as an F0F1-ATPase homolog by Eichelberg, Ginocchio & Galan 1994).

machinery_genes.quote: The length of the needle segment is controlled by the protein InvJ
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; InvJ is the SctP needle-length ruler. Marlovits et al 2006 Nature (PMID 16738660) shows 'Assembly of the inner rod, which is regulated by the InvJ protein, triggers conformational changes ... reprogramming the secretion apparatus to stop secretion of the needle protein'.

machinery_genes.role: gatekeeper controlling translocon vs effector secretion hierarchy
machinery_genes.quote: Salmonella protein InvE, which is also encoded within SPI-1, is essential for the translocation of bacterial proteins into host cells
machinery_genes.note: Kubori & Galan 2002; InvE is the SctW-family gatekeeper. An invE loss-of-function mutant shows reduced secretion of the SipB/SipC/SipD translocases while over-secreting effectors, identifying InvE as the hierarchy-controlling gatekeeper (not an effector or regulator of transcription).

machinery_genes.quote: the protein SipD forms a platform for the translocon composed of the transmembrane proteins SipB and SipC
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; SipB is a transmembrane translocon component forming the host-membrane pore. Per the apparatus brief, translocon proteins are machinery (they form the pore), not effectors. Also documented as a translocase in Kubori & Galan 2002 (PMID 12169593).

machinery_genes.quote: the protein SipD forms a platform for the translocon composed of the transmembrane proteins SipB and SipC
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; SipC is a transmembrane translocon component forming the host-membrane pore. Listed as a translocon (apparatus), not effector, per the apparatus brief. Also documented as a translocase in Kubori & Galan 2002 (PMID 12169593).

machinery_genes.quote: The needle tip structure is capped with SipD, which is secreted by a nascent T3SS filament
machinery_genes.note: Lou 2019; SipD caps the needle tip and forms the platform for the SipB/SipC translocon. Tip-complex protein is apparatus per the brief. Translocase role corroborated by Kubori & Galan 2002 (PMID 12169593).

notes: System = the Salmonella Pathogenicity Island 1 (SPI-1) injectisome T3SS, the archetypal Inv/Spa/Prg/Org system in S. enterica serovar Typhimurium. The apparatus paper strain is S. Typhimurium (Kubori 1998 SJW1103/SB derivatives; Kimbrough 2000 SL1344-derived strains; Kubori & Galan 2002 SB strains), the same SPI-1 system present in LT2, so no cross-system transfer is involved (only minor lab-strain differences within S. Typhimurium). Core apparatus covered gene-by-gene: secretin InvG + pilotin InvH; basal-body inner-membrane rings PrgH (SctD) and PrgK (SctJ); needle PrgI (SctF) and inner rod PrgJ (SctI); export apparatus InvA/SpaP/SpaQ/SpaR/SpaS (SctV/R/S/T/U); sorting platform + ATPase SpaO/OrgA/OrgB/InvI/InvC (SctQ/K/L/O/N); needle-length ruler InvJ (SctP); gatekeeper InvE (SctW); tip/translocon SipD/SipB/SipC. Translocon proteins SipB, SipC, SipD are included as machinery per the T3SS apparatus brief (they form the host-membrane pore and needle tip), despite being secreted. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: secreted effectors (SipA, SopA/B/D/E, AvrA, SptP etc., including this instance's curated effectors); translocated/secretion chaperones (SicA, SicP, InvB, SpaO chaperone fraction); and transcriptional regulators (HilA, HilC, HilD, InvF, RtsA, SprB). Low-confidence note: the InvH naming quote ties InvH to InvG secretin-ring assembly rather than calling InvH a 'structural' subunit outright, but InvH is well established as the SPI-1 secretin pilotin. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PubMed/PMC abstracts or full texts; all five DOIs and PMIDs were confirmed via get_article_metadata.
